spec.reasoning: The instruction explicitly references absolute cell addresses (B6:B13, column E, E6:E13) and the risk flags confirm instruction_references_absolute_rows and task_references_specific_cell_addresses. This means we CANNOT insert blank rows or title rows (which would shift data and break the absolute references). Column reorder is also unsafe because the instruction references column B and column E by letter. Column renaming is risky because 'Description' and 'Type' could be considered common English words contextually tied to the instruction's logic (the instruction talks about what a cell 'contains' and the result 'Type'). The safest perturbations are: (1) adding a distractor column 'Category' after the last column — this is plausibly similar to 'Type' but clearly different, and it doesn't affect any existing column letters or row positions; (2) we avoid paraphrasing per the rules. We keep all column names, sheet names, and data values unchanged to preserve the absolute references in the instruction.
